---
title: "Getting Started with CrossingTools"
output: rmarkdown::html_vignette
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{Getting Started with CrossingTools}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

```{r, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  collapse = TRUE,
  comment = "#>"
)
```

```{r setup, message=FALSE}
library(CrossingTools)

n_ind   <- 20
n_mark  <- 200
n_trait <- 3

# Diploid genotype matrix: 0/2 coding for AA/BB
M  <- matrix(sample(c(0, 2), n_ind * n_mark, replace = TRUE), nrow = n_ind)

# Additive marker effects (markers in rows, traits in columns)
U  <- matrix(rnorm(n_mark * n_trait), ncol = n_trait)

# Simple 2-chromosome map, evenly spaced
map <- data.frame(
  site = 1:n_mark,
  chr  = rep(1:2, each = n_mark/2),
  pos  = c(seq(0, 100, length.out = n_mark/2),
           seq(0, 100, length.out = n_mark/2))
)

# Create pairwise crosses (scales as O(n^2))
crosses <- t(combn(n_ind, 2))
```

### Doubled haploids (DH)
```{r dh}
# We compute for doubled haploid (DH) families.
# method can be "osthushenrich" or "lehermeier".
# With multiple traits, setting covariance = TRUE returns trait covariances for segregation.
out_dh <- calculate_variances_DH(
  crosses         = crosses,   # (n_crosses x 2)
  genetic.map     = map,       # must contain site/chr/pos and cover all M columns
  M               = M,         # 0/2 genotype coding
  U               = U,         # additive marker effects (markers x traits)
  t               = 2,         # random-mating generations before DH creation
  intensity       = 1,         # selection differential for SPV
  covariance      = TRUE,      # also return segregation covariance matrices
  gains           = rep(10, n_trait),
  calculate.gains = TRUE,
  method          = "osthushenrich"
)

# cross_values is a data.frame with columns like: EGBV1, var1, SPV1, EGBV2, ...
head(out_dh$cross_values)

# If covariance = TRUE, out_dh also contains covariance matrices per cross in out_dh$covariances.
# Show the covariance for the first cross (trait x trait matrix)
out_dh$covariances[[1]]

# If covariance = TRUE and calculate.gains = TRUE, out_dh$gains is a data.frame with
# desired-gains summaries like EGBV_DG, var_DG, SPV_DG.
head(out_dh$gains)
```

### Recombinant inbred lines (RIL)
```{r ril}
out_ril <- calculate_variances_RIL(
  crosses         = crosses,   # (n_crosses x 2)
  genetic.map     = map,       # must contain site/chr/pos and cover all M columns
  M               = M,         # 0/2 genotype coding
  U               = U,         # additive marker effects (markers x traits)
  t               = 2,         # random-mating generations before RIL creation
  intensity       = 1,         # selection differential for SPV
  covariance      = TRUE,      # also return segregation covariance matrices
  gains           = rep(10, n_trait),
  calculate.gains = TRUE,
  method          = "osthushenrich"
)

head(out_ril$cross_values)
# If covariance = TRUE, out_ril also contains covariance matrices per cross in out_ril$covariances.
out_ril$covariances[[1]]
# If covariance = TRUE and calculate.gains = TRUE, out_ril$gains has desired-gains summaries.
head(out_ril$gains)
```

### Four-way crosses (DH & RIL)
```{r four-way}
# Build 4-way crosses by column-binding two 2-way crosses:
#   columns 1:2 = first pair, columns 3:4 = second pair.
# For a 3-way cross, repeat the first pair in columns 1:2 and provide distinct 3rd and 4th as needed.
# Note: everything that is possible with the two functions above is also possible here.
# There is also an RIL equivalent for this one.

crosses4 <- cbind(crosses, crosses)  # toy example: just duplicate the pair

out_4w_DH <- calculate_variances_4W_DH(
  crosses     = crosses4,  # (n_crosses x 4)
  genetic.map = map,
  M           = M,
  U           = U,
  t           = 1,
  intensity   = 1,
  covariance  = TRUE
)

out_4w_RIL <- calculate_variances_4W_RIL(
  crosses     = crosses4,  # (n_crosses x 4)
  genetic.map = map,
  M           = M,
  U           = U,
  t           = 1,
  intensity   = 1,
  covariance  = TRUE
)

head(out_4w_DH$cross_values)
head(out_4w_RIL$cross_values)
```

### Heterozygous parents and F1 (additive + dominance)
```{r f1}
# For heterozygous parents you provide haplotypes for each individual:
# hap1 and hap2 use 0/1 coding and have the same number of rows (individuals) and columns (markers).
hap1 <- matrix(sample(0:1, n_ind * n_mark, replace = TRUE), nrow = n_ind, ncol = n_mark)
hap2 <- matrix(sample(0:1, n_ind * n_mark, replace = TRUE), nrow = n_ind, ncol = n_mark)

# Dominance marker effects (markers in rows, traits in columns)
D <- matrix(rnorm(n_mark * n_trait), ncol = n_trait)

out_f1 <- calculate_variances_F1(
  crosses         = crosses,   # same cross list
  genetic.map     = map,
  hap1            = hap1,      # 0/1 haplotypes
  hap2            = hap2,      # 0/1 haplotypes
  U               = U,         # additive effects
  D               = D,         # dominance effects
  intensity       = 1,
  calculate.gains = TRUE,
  gains           = c(1, 1, 1),
  covariance      = TRUE
)

# cross_values contains columns: EGBV, ETGV, var_a, SPV, var_d, TSPV (per trait)
# Similar output shape: cross_values + optional covariances (additive and dominance)
head(out_f1$cross_values)
head(out_f1$gains)
out_f1$additive_covariances
```

### Optimal cross selection (OCS)
```{r ocs}
# We need a relationship matrix G to quantify diversity.

G <- tcrossprod(scale(M, center = TRUE, scale = FALSE)) / n_mark

# Choose a cross-level criterion; here we use SPV for a focal trait.
crit <- out_dh$cross_values$SPV1  # pick trait 1 SPV from DH results

# Run OCS to select a set of crosses balancing gain (crit) and diversity (G).
# The GA parameters below are conservative to keep the vignette quick; increase for real problems.
ocs <- optimal_cross_selection(
  crosses        = crosses,
  ncrosses       = 20,                # number of crosses to choose
  G              = G,                 # relationship / kinship matrix
  u              = crit,              # cross-level criterion (length = nrow(crosses))
  target.angle   = 30 * pi / 180,     # 30 degrees (in radians) between max gain and min similarity
  nselect        = 200,               # GA parent pool size
  npop           = 1000,              # GA population size
  max.generation = 200,               # GA iterations
  max.iteration  = 50,                # early-stopping tolerance
  angle.penalty  = 0.01,              # penalize deviation from target angle
  nthreads       = 2L                 # use more threads in your environment if available
)

# The selected cross plan is returned as (ncrosses x 2)
head(ocs$crossPlan)
```

### Other utilities
```{r ohv}
# The OHV function can work with blocks of markers; if no blocks are provided,
# each marker is treated as its own block. Here we use additive effects for traits.
ohv <- calculate_optimal_haploid_value(
  crosses = crosses,  # (n_crosses x 2)
  M       = M,        # genotype matrix
  U       = U         # trait matrix (n_mark x n_trait)
)

head(ohv)
```

```{r backsolve}
# Backsolving marker effects
# Suppose you have individual-level genetic values g (n_ind x n_trait) and want marker effects U.
# We use a toy g here; in practice, g might come from BLUP/EBV/GBLUP solutions.
g_vals <- matrix(rnorm(n_ind * n_trait), ncol = n_trait)

u_fit <- u_from_g(
  M                 = M,           # markers (n_ind x n_mark)
  G                 = G,           # relationship matrix used in your model
  g                 = g_vals,      # individual genetic values (n_ind x n_trait)
  scalingFactor     = 1 / n_mark,  # must match how you formed G
  tol               = 1e-10,       # tolerance for inversions
  LDvar             = TRUE,        # return LD-inclusive variance by groups (if grouping specified)
  calcPriorVcov     = FALSE,       # don't compute prior Vcov among markers
  calcPosteriorVcov = FALSE        # don't compute posterior Vcov from PEV
)

# u_fit$mu_matrix contains marker effects (n_mark x n_trait)
head(u_fit$mu_matrix)

# If LDvar = TRUE without grouping, a single-group variance is returned per trait.
# Provide a 'Grouping' data.frame with a 'chr' column for chromosome-wise variance.
u_fit$variance_LD
```
